name: Kong Konnect MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Kong Konnect MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Kong Konnect's API management platform — managing API gateways, services, routes, plugins, consumers, credentials, and monitoring API traffic across Kong's cloud-managed or hybrid infrastructure.
use_cases: Managing API routes and services from infrastructure automation agents
Configuring rate limiting and authentication plugins from security agents
Monitoring API traffic and performance metrics from observability agents
Managing API consumers and credentials from access control agents
Deploying new API definitions from CI/CD pipeline agents
Querying service catalog for API discovery agents
not_for: Teams using AWS API Gateway, Azure APIM, or Apigee
Teams using self-managed Kong without Konnect cloud
best_when: An agent needs to manage APIs and microservice traffic in a Kong Konnect-powered environment.
avoid_when: You're using AWS API Gateway, Azure APIM, or another API gateway platform.

auth.notes: Kong Konnect Personal Access Tokens (PAT) or API keys. System accounts for automation. Role-based access control with Konnect roles.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: limited API calls and services
pricing.notes: Kong Konnect is the managed version. Kong Gateway OSS is free open source. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: Konnect API: rate limits documented per endpoint

known_agent_gotchas: Control plane vs data plane distinction — management API is control plane
Service, route, and plugin IDs are UUIDs — agents must discover before modifying
Plugin configuration schemas vary by plugin type — inspect before configuring
Runtime groups (KRG) concept in Konnect — agents must understand topology
API versioning in Konnect uses date-based versions
System accounts vs personal tokens have different permissions and rotation requirements
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. PAT with RBAC roles. FedRAMP, SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR. Enterprise security controls.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. RBAC roles. FedRAMP, SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR. Enterprise controls.
